teamObjective.explanation: Opening lead while an opponent is near finish (player 1 has only 2 cards). Leading the lowest single tries to make opponents spend a higher card to take control, slowing the short-stacked opponent without committing my own meager resources.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 cards left, but their ranks, suits, and strengths are unknown.
evidence: publicHistory only contains new_game, so no cardplay inferences are available

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 is near finish with only 2 cards; the exact cards are unknown. Other opponents have 3 cards each with unknown composition.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the lowest single (club 3) is the most resource-efficient opening. It commits the smallest card, gives partner the best chance to win the trick with a modest card, and may force the short-stacked opponent to burn a stronger card if they want the lead.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Only slightly stronger than the 3; still easily beaten and wastes a marginally higher card.
Commits a middle card with no real chance of surviving a trick; better kept for later if I regain the lead.
My highest card, but still a weak non-trump; leading it uses my best remaining card without making the trick meaningfully harder for opponents to win.

risks: A 3 of clubs can be beaten by nearly any higher card or any trump, so I will likely lose the lead.
If the short-stacked opponent wins the trick, they gain control and may finish their hand soon.
riskAssessment.mitigation: My hand is entirely low non-trumps, so no lead can avoid being beaten. Leading the 3 preserves the 4-6 for any later lead opportunities and asks partner to help take control.
